---
title: "UCI Basic Needs — CalFresh Analysis"
resources:
  - report.pdf
---

```{=html}
<style>
h1.title {font-size: 1.6rem; margin-top: 2.5rem;}
.project-header {margin: 0 0 2rem 0;}
.back-link {font-size: 13px; color: #888; text-decoration: none; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; margin-bottom: 1.5rem;}
.back-link:hover {color: #444;}
.project-subtitle {font-size: 0.95rem; color: #666; margin-bottom: 1.5rem;}
.meta-row {display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 2rem;}
.meta-tag {font-size: 13px; padding: 3px 10px; border-radius: 20px; background: #f5f5f5; color: #555; border: 1px solid #e8e8e8;}
.metrics-grid {display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 12px; margin-bottom: 2rem;}
.metric-card {background: #f9f9f9; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1rem 1.25rem; text-align: center;}
.metric-value {font-size: 1.6rem; font-weight: 700; color: #1a1a1a; line-height: 1.2;}
.metric-label {font-size: 12px; color: #444; margin-top: 4px; line-height: 1.4;}
.overview-text {font-size: 0.95rem; color: #444; line-height: 1.5; margin-bottom: 1.25rem;}
.bq-block {font-size: 0.95rem; color: #444; line-height: 1.5; margin-bottom: 1.25rem;}
.intro-divider {border: none; border-top: 1px solid #e5e5e5; margin: 0 0 2rem 0;}
.tabs {display: flex; gap: 0; margin-bottom: 0; border-bottom: 2px solid #e5e5e5;}
.tab-btn {font-size: 15px; font-weight: 400; color: #888; background: none; border: none; padding: 12px 24px; cursor: pointer; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; margin-bottom: -2px; transition: color 0.15s; font-family: inherit;}
.tab-btn:hover {color: #444;}
.tab-btn.active {color: #1a1a1a; border-bottom-color: #1a1a1a; font-weight: 500;}
.tab-panel {display: none; padding: 2rem 0;}
.tab-panel.active {display: block;}
.section-heading {font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1a1a1a; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; margin-top: 1.5rem;}
.section-heading:first-child {margin-top: 0;}
.prose {font-size: 0.93rem; color: #444; line-height: 1.5; margin-bottom: 1rem;}
.findings-list {list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0.5rem 0 1.25rem 0;}
.findings-list li {font-size: 0.93rem; color: #444; line-height: 1.5; padding: 7px 0 7px 18px; position: relative; border-bottom: 1px solid #f5f5f5;}
.findings-list li:last-child {border-bottom: none;}
.findings-list li::before {content: '•'; position: absolute; left: 0; color: #aaa; font-size: 14px;}
.rec-card {border-left: 4px solid #e8e8e8; padding: 1.1rem 1.25rem; margin-bottom: 16px; background: #fafafa; border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0;}
.rec-card.high {border-left-color: #e24b4a;}
.rec-card.med {border-left-color: #ef9f27;}
.rec-card.low {border-left-color: #639922;}
.rec-priority {font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 6px;}
.rec-card.high .rec-priority {color: #e24b4a;}
.rec-card.med .rec-priority {color: #ef9f27;}
.rec-card.low .rec-priority {color: #639922;}
.rec-title {font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: #1a1a1a; margin-bottom: 5px;}
.rec-desc {font-size: 13px; color: #444; line-height: 1.5;}
.method-row {display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr; gap: 12px; margin-bottom: 1.5rem;}
.method-step {background: #f9f9f9; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1rem 1.25rem; border: 1px solid #efefef;}
.step-num {font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #aaa; text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 4px;}
.step-title {font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; color: #1a1a1a; margin-bottom: 6px;}
.step-desc {font-size: 14px; color: #555; line-height: 1.5;}
.tools-list {list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0.5rem 0 1.5rem 0;}
.tools-list li {font-size: 0.93rem; color: #444; line-height: 1.5; padding: 9px 0; border-bottom: 1px solid #f0f0f0;}
.tools-list li:last-child {border-bottom: none;}
.tools-list li strong {color: #1a1a1a;}
.takeaway-list {list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; counter-reset: takeaway-counter;}
.takeaway-list li {font-size: 0.93rem; color: #444; line-height: 1.5; padding: 10px 0 10px 28px; border-bottom: 1px solid #f0f0f0; position: relative; counter-increment: takeaway-counter;}
.takeaway-list li::before {content: counter(takeaway-counter); position: absolute; left: 0; top: 12px; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; color: #aaa; background: #f0f0f0; border-radius: 50%; width: 17px; height: 17px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; line-height: 1;}
.takeaway-list li:last-child {border-bottom: none;}
.tableau-breakout {width: 100vw; position: relative; left: 50%; right: 50%; margin-left: -50vw; margin-right: -50vw; padding: 0; display: flex; justify-content: center; margin-bottom: 2.5rem;}
.tableau-wrap {width: 800px; border: 1px solid #e8e8e8; border-radius: 10px; overflow: hidden;}
.tableau-note {font-size: 0.95rem; color: #444; line-height: 1.5; margin-bottom: 1.5rem;}
.glossary {background: #f9f9f9; border: 1px solid #e8e8e8; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem; margin-bottom: 1rem;}
.glossary-title {font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; color: #888; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; margin-bottom: 1rem;}
.glossary-entry {display: grid; grid-template-columns: 150px 1fr; gap: 8px 20px; padding: 0.65rem 0; border-bottom: 1px solid #f0f0f0;}
.glossary-entry:last-child {border-bottom: none; padding-bottom: 0;}
.glossary-term {font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; color: #333;}
.glossary-def {font-size: 13px; color: #555; line-height: 1.5;}
@media (max-width: 600px) {.glossary-entry {grid-template-columns: 1fr;} .metrics-grid {grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;}}
</style>

<div class="project-header">
  <a class="back-link" href="../../projects.html">← Back to Projects</a>
  <div class="project-subtitle">UCI Basic Needs Center · Data Visualization · 2025–2026</div>
  <div class="meta-row">
    <span class="meta-tag">Excel</span>
    <span class="meta-tag">Tableau</span>
    <span class="meta-tag">Program Evaluation</span>
    <span class="meta-tag">Data Visualization</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="metrics-grid">
  <div class="metric-card">
    <div class="metric-value">6,261</div>
    <div class="metric-label">Appointment records analyzed</div>
  </div>
  <div class="metric-card">
    <div class="metric-value">4 yrs</div>
    <div class="metric-label">Jan 2022 – Feb 2026</div>
  </div>
  <div class="metric-card">
    <div class="metric-value">94.3%</div>
    <div class="metric-label">Students with at least one eligibility qualifier</div>
  </div>
</div>

<p class="overview-text"><strong>Project Context:</strong> I previously worked as a Program Coordinator at UCI's Basic Needs Center, where part of my role involved analyzing CalFresh assistance appointment data in Excel — looking for patterns, identifying underrepresented communities, and finding opportunities to improve communication with students. After completing coursework in the MSBA program, I reached out to my former supervisor and asked if I could revisit the data with a fresh set of tools and skills. The goal was to build an interactive dashboard that the team could actually use — something they could present, share, and maintain over time to track the performance of their CalFresh assistance services and Enrollment Party events. While the project includes KPIs and recommendations, the broader aim was to give program leadership a clearer, data-driven picture of where their CalFresh initiatives stand. <em>For more on my time at the Basic Needs Center, visit the <a href="../../experience.html">Experience</a> page.</em></p>

<p class="bq-block"><strong>Business Question:</strong> <em>How is the UCI Basic Needs Center's CalFresh assistance program performing over time, and what operational changes would most improve student access and staff efficiency?</em></p>

<div class="glossary">
  <div class="glossary-title">Key Terms</div>
  <div class="glossary-entry"><span class="glossary-term">CalFresh</span><span class="glossary-def">California's name for the federal Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP). The program issues monthly benefits on an EBT card — up to $298/month — that can be used at most grocery stores and farmers markets. Eligibility is determined by Orange County Social Services. The application process can be confusing and overwhelming for students, which is why the Basic Needs Center offers one-on-one assistance to guide them through it.</span></div>
  <div class="glossary-entry"><span class="glossary-term">EBT Card</span><span class="glossary-def">Electronic Benefits Transfer card. The physical card loaded with CalFresh benefits each month. Students apply for CalFresh and, if approved, receive an EBT card they can use like a debit card at eligible retailers.</span></div>
  <div class="glossary-entry"><span class="glossary-term">CalFresh Assistance Appointment</span><span class="glossary-def">A one-on-one appointment offered by the UCI Basic Needs Center where a trained advocate helps a student apply for CalFresh, complete a SAR7, or submit a recertification. Appointments can be virtual (Zoom) or in-person. <em>This is the primary unit of analysis in this project.</em></span></div>
  <div class="glossary-entry"><span class="glossary-term">Initial Application</span><span class="glossary-def">A student's first CalFresh application. During the appointment, the advocate explains what CalFresh is, walks through the different eligibility pathways, helps gather required documents, applies alongside the student, and explains next steps in the county review process.</span></div>
  <div class="glossary-entry"><span class="glossary-term">Enrollment Party (EP)</span><span class="glossary-def">A day-long event hosted by the Basic Needs Center where students can apply, get approved, and receive their EBT card in a single visit — bypassing the typical 30-day county processing timeline. EP events are designed to maximize throughput and reduce barriers for students who cannot navigate the standard process alone.</span></div>
  <div class="glossary-entry"><span class="glossary-term">Semi-Annual Report (SAR7)</span><span class="glossary-def">A required county check-in submitted 6 months into a CalFresh certification period. Students must report any changes in income, household size, or other eligibility factors. Failure to submit results in benefits being discontinued.</span></div>
  <div class="glossary-entry"><span class="glossary-term">Recertification (RE)</span><span class="glossary-def">The annual renewal of CalFresh benefits at the end of a certification period. Similar to the SAR7 but more comprehensive. The standard CalFresh lifecycle follows: Initial Application → SAR7 → RE → SAR7 → RE, repeating until the student is no longer eligible.</span></div>
  <div class="glossary-entry"><span class="glossary-term">Student Eligibilities</span><span class="glossary-def">In addition to meeting basic income and household requirements, college students must meet at least one student-specific eligibility criterion to qualify for CalFresh. It is the role of the Basic Needs advocate during a CalFresh assistance appointment to explain the different eligibility pathways and identify which ones apply to each student.</span></div>
  <div class="glossary-entry"><span class="glossary-term">No-Show</span><span class="glossary-def">A student who had a scheduled CalFresh assistance appointment but did not attend and did not cancel in advance. No-shows represent wasted advocate capacity and are a key operational challenge for the program.</span></div>
  <div class="glossary-entry"><span class="glossary-term">LPIE</span><span class="glossary-def">Local Programs that Increase Employability. Specific majors and programs at UCI approved by the California Department of Social Services as containing an employment and training component. Students enrolled in an LPIE-approved program are automatically exempt from the standard CalFresh student eligibility rule — meaning enrollment alone qualifies them, with no additional work or income requirements. LPIE is the most common eligibility qualifier in this dataset at 73.5%.</span></div>
</div>

<hr class="intro-divider">

<div class="section-heading" style="margin-bottom:0.4rem;">Interactive Dashboard</div>
<p class="tableau-note">Please use the Overview, Program Trends, and Student Profile buttons at the top of the dashboard to switch between pages.</p>

<div class="tableau-breakout">
<div class="tableau-wrap">
<div class='tableauPlaceholder' id='viz1775100456113' style='position: relative'><noscript><a href='#'><img alt='Overview' src='https://public.tableau.com/static/images/BN/BNVisuals/ProgramTrends/1_rss.png' style='border: none' /></a></noscript><object class='tableauViz' style='display:none;'><param name='host_url' value='https%3A%2F%2Fpublic.tableau.com%2F' /><param name='embed_code_version' value='3' /><param name='site_root' value='' /><param name='name' value='BNVisuals/Overview' /><param name='tabs' value='no' /><param name='toolbar' value='yes' /><param name='static_image' value='https://public.tableau.com/static/images/BN/BNVisuals/ProgramTrends/1.png' /><param name='animate_transition' value='yes' /><param name='display_static_image' value='yes' /><param name='display_spinner' value='yes' /><param name='display_overlay' value='yes' /><param name='display_count' value='yes' /><param name='language' value='en-US' /><param name='filter' value='publish=yes' /></object></div><script type='text/javascript'>var divElement = document.getElementById('viz1775100456113'); var vizElement = divElement.getElementsByTagName('object')[0]; if ( divElement.offsetWidth > 800 ) { vizElement.style.width='800px';vizElement.style.height='627px';} else if ( divElement.offsetWidth > 500 ) { vizElement.style.width='800px';vizElement.style.height='627px';} else { vizElement.style.width='100%';vizElement.style.height='977px';} var scriptElement = document.createElement('script'); scriptElement.src = 'https://public.tableau.com/javascripts/api/viz_v1.js'; vizElement.parentNode.insertBefore(scriptElement, vizElement);</script>
</div>
</div>


<div class="tabs">
  <button class="tab-btn active" onclick="showTab('findings', this)">Findings</button>
  <button class="tab-btn" onclick="showTab('recommendations', this)">Recommendations</button>
  <button class="tab-btn" onclick="showTab('methodology', this)">Methodology</button>
  <button class="tab-btn" onclick="showTab('takeaways', this)">Takeaways</button>
</div>

<div id="findings" class="tab-panel active">
  <div class="section-heading">What the Data Shows</div>
 <ul class="findings-list">
    <li><strong>October demand is 2x any other month</strong> — averaging ~194 appointments, mapping directly to Fall quarter start and financial aid renewal cycles. This is not new information for advocates, who have always experienced a heavier workload in the fall, but the data puts a concrete number to what the team already knows anecdotally.</li>
    <li><strong>The EP program has overtaken individual appointments</strong> — students served at Enrollment Party events grew from 84 in Fall 2022 to 572 in Fall 2025, crossing over individual appointment volume for the first time. Given the trajectory, EP events are expected to continue pulling further ahead as the program scales.</li>
    <li><strong>No-show and cancellation rates have remained flat</strong> — hovering between 11–21% per quarter despite overall program growth, representing roughly 208 missed appointments per year. This is a useful baseline, and the data now exists to measure whether any future intervention actually moves the needle.</li>
    <li><strong>94.3% of students served have at least one eligibility qualifier</strong> — LPIE is the most common at 73.5%. This is partly a reflection of how much the LPIE list has expanded, with many programs added in 2023 and 2024. The practical implication is that eligibility is no longer the main barrier. The bigger challenge now is simply awareness — most students qualify but have no idea they do.</li>
    <li><strong>Fall 2025 EP approval rate decreased</strong> — dipping below the 86.8% average, but this should be read in context. EP events are serving dramatically more students than in prior years, and a slight decline in approval rate is a natural byproduct of reaching a broader, less pre-screened population. Volume gains more than offset the rate dip.</li>
    <li><strong>Zoom appointments are declining year over year</strong> — from 88.9% in 2022 to 74.3% in 2026. This likely reflects both post-COVID comfort with in-person services and the nature of the process itself. CalFresh applications involve gathering documents, reviewing eligibility, and navigating county systems, and some students may simply feel they need in-person support to get through it.</li>
  </ul>
</div>

<div id="recommendations" class="tab-panel">
  <p class="prose">Placeholder text — we'll refine this together.</p>

  <div class="rec-card high">
    <div class="rec-priority">High Priority</div>
    <div class="rec-title">Staff Up for Fall Quarter Demand Surge</div>
    <div class="rec-desc">October appointment volume is 2x any other month. Proactive staffing increases and targeted outreach before Fall quarter can reduce wait times and prevent students from missing their application window.</div>
  </div>

  <div class="rec-card high">
    <div class="rec-priority">High Priority</div>
    <div class="rec-title">Continue Scaling Enrollment Party Events</div>
    <div class="rec-desc">EP events now serve more students per staff hour than individual appointments. Continuing to invest in EP events is the highest-leverage action available for increasing total students served without proportional staffing increases.</div>
  </div>

  <div class="rec-card med">
    <div class="rec-priority">Medium Priority</div>
    <div class="rec-title">Implement SMS Appointment Reminders</div>
    <div class="rec-desc">No-show and cancellation rates have remained flat at 13–16% for four years. A lightweight SMS confirmation and reminder system could meaningfully reduce the ~208 missed appointments per year and free up significant staff capacity.</div>
  </div>

  <div class="rec-card med">
    <div class="rec-priority">Medium Priority</div>
    <div class="rec-title">Investigate Fall 2025 EP Approval Rate Drop</div>
    <div class="rec-desc">The EP approval rate fell to ~55% in Fall 2025 against an 86.8% average. Auditing those events for changes in the approval process, student eligibility mix, or documentation requirements can determine whether this is a one-time anomaly or an emerging trend.</div>
  </div>

  <div class="rec-card low">
    <div class="rec-priority">Lower Priority</div>
    <div class="rec-title">Plan for Continued In-Person Growth</div>
    <div class="rec-desc">Zoom appointments have declined from 88.9% to 74.3% over four years. Maintaining hybrid infrastructure while planning for continued in-person growth will keep the program accessible as student preferences continue to shift.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div id="methodology" class="tab-panel">
  <div class="method-row">
    <div class="method-step">
      <div class="step-num">Step 1</div>
      <div class="step-title">Data Collection and Cleaning</div>
      <div class="step-desc">The dataset contains 6,261 CalFresh appointment records spanning January 2022 to February 2026, exported from the Basic Needs Center's appointment management system. Data was cleaned in Excel, including standardizing appointment type labels, removing duplicate entries, and flagging records with missing values in newly added demographic columns.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="method-step">
      <div class="step-num">Step 2</div>
      <div class="step-title">Data Caveats</div>
      <div class="step-desc">Five demographic columns (Working, Language Barriers, Have Children Under 18, Military Veteran, 60+) were added to data collection around October 2024 and cover only 1,829 of 6,261 records. Findings from these fields reflect recent cohorts only. Seven eligibility dummy variables indicate whether a student qualifies under each criterion — a student needs only one to be eligible. Enrollment Party event counts are tracked separately and are not directly comparable to individual appointment counts.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="method-step">
      <div class="step-num">Step 3</div>
      <div class="step-title">Visualization in Tableau</div>
      <div class="step-desc">All charts were built in Tableau Desktop and published to Tableau Public. The dashboard is organized across three pages with clickable navigation buttons: Overview (KPI cards, appointment type breakdown, seasonality), Program Trends (CF vs EP over time, no-show and cancellation trends, year-over-year comparison), and Student Profile (EP approval rate, county distribution, eligibility breakdown).</div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div id="takeaways" class="tab-panel">
  <div class="section-heading">What I took away from this project</div>
  <ul class="takeaway-list">
    <li>Working with real program data from a place I had direct experience in changed how I thought about the analysis. I knew the context behind the numbers, which made it easier to identify which findings were actionable and which were artifacts of data collection timing.</li>
    <li>Seasonality patterns are easy to overlook in summary statistics. The October spike only became visible when I broke appointments down by month rather than looking at annual totals.</li>
    <li>The EP vs individual appointments crossover in Fall 2025 was one of the most meaningful findings in the data, but it was not obvious until I plotted both series on the same chart. Visualization revealed what the raw numbers obscured.</li>
    <li>Placeholder — more takeaways go here.</li>
  </ul>

  <div class="section-heading" style="margin-top:2rem;">Tools and Skills</div>
  <ul class="tools-list">
    <li><strong>Excel</strong>: used for data cleaning, exploratory analysis, and preparing the dataset for Tableau.</li>
    <li><strong>Tableau</strong>: used to build all visualizations and the multi-page interactive dashboard, published to Tableau Public.</li>
    <li><strong>Program Evaluation</strong>: applied to connect data findings to real operational decisions rather than reporting descriptive statistics in isolation.</li>
    <li><strong>Data Storytelling</strong>: structured the dashboard and written analysis to communicate to a non-technical program leadership audience.</li>
  </ul>
</div>

<script>
function showTab(id, btn) {
  document.querySelectorAll('.tab-panel').forEach(p => p.classList.remove('active'));
  document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active'));
  document.getElementById(id).classList.add('active');
  btn.classList.add('active');
}
</script>
```
